technique: Tab character (%09) with $() subshell — WAF regex targets \n but not tab as separator
Semicolon in plain form — testing if WAF decodes before or after regex match; semicolon is in block list but trying literal form
Double-URL-encoded && operator — %26%26 may bypass regex [;&|`$()\n] since & is not in the block list and %26 may not be decoded before WAF check
https scheme with whitelisted host 127.0.0.1 — WAF allows both https scheme and 127.0.0.1 host, combining both to reach internal port
https + whitelisted host 'localhost' to attempt local file-like path fetch — tests if server-side fetches localhost paths
Credential-embedded URL — 127.0.0.1 appears as username so host check passes, actual target is evil.com; tests if WAF host-checks userinfo vs netloc
URL-encoded forward slashes for path traversal — WAF blocks '..' substring but payload uses %2F so raw string may not contain '..'
Double-dot obfuscation with '....//'' — after WAF strips '..', remaining string collapses to '../' traversal; classic WAF evasion for substring checks
